thread.title: JJ Singh wins democratic nomination for House District 26 in firehouse primary
thread.title_full: JJ Singh wins democratic nomination for House District 26 in firehouse primary

author: 7News Staff
title: JJ Singh wins democratic nomination for House District 26 in firehouse primary
text: JJ Singh won the Democratic nomination for Loudoun County’s House District 26 seat following a firehouse primary on Saturday. Singh beat four other candidates who ran for the Democratic nomination by nearly 300 votes and will face off against Republican Ram Venkatachalam in a Jan. 7 special election. READ MORE | Alcohol believed to be a factor in fatal high-speed Reston crash, police say Since Democrats hold a one-seat majority in both the Virginia State Senate and the House of Delegates, this special election in Loudoun County will determine which party holds control in Richmond. On Nov. 20, Singh announced he received Delegate Kannan Srinivasan’s endorsement. RELATED | Five Loudoun County Democrats face off in a Saturday primary for January special election \"Kannan and I have known each other, and he's a very professional, very hard-working guy, very thorough and effective legislator, and I admire the work that he's done, and I think he sees some of those things in me,\" Singh said. \"I'm a very diligent, hard-working guy, and I think he recognizes that these are some tough times, and tough times calls for tough people.\" Srinivasan represented House District 26 until he resigned this week to run for State Senate, which is triggering the special election for House District 26. Watch 7News' Nick Minock's seven-minute interview with Singh on X.
